architecture.location: CLAUDE.md:82 (Architecture, Rendering bullet)
architecture.claim: "zero UI-thread `BestSession.For` / `AltAz.At` / `AstroUtil.GetMoonAltitude` calls in any cache-warm path (defensive inline fallbacks exist in moon paths for cache-miss races, log WARN if they fire)."
architecture.finding: The Sky chart calls AltAzCalculator.At per-minute on the UI thread during every Render (the K-S brightness walk) — this is an unconditional inline AltAz.At call in a cache-warm path, not a fallback. The 'zero AltAz.At' assertion is overstated. (BestSession.For and the moon-path WARN-fallback claims do hold.)
architecture.evidence: AltitudeSubChart_Sky.cs:509 `AltAz t = AltAzCalculator.At(target, location, utc);` inside BuildOrUpdateTargetSeries, invoked synchronously per target from the Render loop (Sky.cs:383-385), per-minute over `count` samples (Sky.cs:503-509). The code's own comment (Sky.cs:369) disclaims only 'zero BestSession.For calls in the Sky render path' — it does not claim zero AltAz.At. Inline AltAz here is intentional/correct (cheap Meeus, needed for per-minute airmass), so the doc — not the code — is the inaccurate side.
